wordmark.purpose: HARDWIRE brand lockup on the amber bar. Hero-level only.

tagline.purpose: Locked product line above the brand bar.

headline.purpose: Summary section titles below the fold.

title.purpose: Compact HUD titles on orbit cards and consoles.

body.purpose: Supporting mono copy; keep measures tight (~52ch).

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked micro labels and status metadata.

shadows.name: acquire-hub
shadows.purpose: Harness hub presence on the globe.

motion.name: globe-spin
motion.purpose: Wireframe globe rotation; disabled under reduced motion.

components.name: Display Card
components.description: Orbit HUD instrument card: hard border, console fill, pixel title + mono body.
components.css: .ds-display-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; width: 6.5rem; padding: 4px 6px; background: var(--hw-card, #0c0c0c); color: #f2f2f0; border: 1px solid rgba(242,242,240,0.9); border-radius: 0; text-align: left; } .ds-display-card-title { margin: 0; font-family: var(--font-geist-pixel-square), monospace; font-size: 0.6875rem; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1; } .ds-display-card-body { margin: 0; font-family: var(--font-geist-mono), ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.5625rem; font-weight: 300; letter-spacing: -0.01em; line-height: 1.15; color: rgba(242,242,240,0.9); } .ds-display-card:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e8a020; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Mission Console
components.description: Edge-docked use-case trigger; bleeds off the right viewport edge.
components.css: .ds-mission-console { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: flex-start; gap: 4px; min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 16px 8px 12px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.7); color: #f2f2f0; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4); border-right: 0; border-radius: 0; text-align: left; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.2s, opacity 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; opacity: 0.75; } .ds-mission-console:hover { border-color: #e8a020; opacity: 1; } .ds-mission-console[aria-pressed="true"] { border-color: #e8a020; opacity: 1; box-shadow: 0 0 18px rgba(232,160,32,0.25); } .ds-mission-console:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e8a020; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-mission-meta { font-family: var(--font-geist-mono), ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.5625rem; letter-spacing: 0.18em; color: rgba(242,242,240,0.6); } .ds-mission-title { font-family: var(--font-geist-pixel-square), monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-mission-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: var(--font-geist-mono), ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.5625rem; letter-spacing: 0.14em; color: rgba(232,160,32,0.9); } .ds-mission-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #e8a020; }

components.name: Brand Bar
components.description: Industrial amber drench with Motorblock wordmark and geometric emblem.
components.css: .ds-brand-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 12px; width: 100%; padding: 0.875rem clamp(1rem, 2.24vw, 2.6875rem); background: var(--hw-orange, #e8a020); color: var(--hw-ink, #0a0a0a); border-radius: 0; } .ds-brand-wordmark { font-family: var(--font-motorblock), sans-serif; font-size: clamp(2rem, 2.8vw + 1rem, 3.5rem); line-height: 0.88; letter-spacing: 0.01em; } .ds-brand-mark { display: block; width: 2rem; height: 2.25rem; background: #0a0a0a; clip-path: polygon(50% 0%, 100% 18%, 100% 100%, 0% 100%, 0% 18%); }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Hard-edged capability tag for mission overlays.
components.css: .ds-status-chip { display: inline-block; padding: 2px 6px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.35); color: rgba(255,255,255,0.85); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.35); border-radius: 0; font-family: var(--font-geist-mono), ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.5625rem; letter-spacing: 0.06em; }

components.name: Harness Hub
components.description: Center aggregation mark on the globe: amber field, ink emblem, acquire glow.
components.css: .ds-harness-hub { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 1.75rem; height: 1.75rem; background: var(--hw-orange, #e8a020); border: 1px solid var(--hw-ink, #0a0a0a); border-radius: 0; box-shadow: 0 0 14px oklch(0.72 0.16 75 / 0.35); } .ds-harness-hub-inner { width: 0.7rem; height: 0.85rem; background: #0a0a0a; clip-path: polygon(50% 0%, 100% 18%, 100% 100%, 0% 100%, 0% 18%); }

narrative.overview:
Hardwire's visual system is a soft-launch investor surface that must read in seconds as mission-grade physical AI infrastructure, not a product brochure. The page behaves like an operations console: black stage, instrumented density, industrial amber as the only saturated signal. Typography is pixel + mono instrumentation paired with a sharp Motorblock wordmark. Every surface should feel bolted on, evidence-first, and closer to Anduril / Palantir / defense-industrial systems than consumer SaaS.

Density is intentional. Prefer tight groupings and precise spacing over airy marketing whitespace. The amber brand bar is a committed drench at the base of the first viewport; the stage above stays void-black and instrumental. Depth comes from hard borders and tonal layering (void → console card → phosphor text), not soft shadows or glass stacks. Amber glow is reserved for acquire, focus, and active console states.

This system explicitly rejects soft SaaS marketing (purple gradients, rounded card grids, friendly illustrations), neon cyberpunk / Matrix cosplay, editorial magazine / Klim-specimen landing aesthetics, "AI wrapper" consumer product pages, and playful startup whimsy (emojis, pill clusters, stat strips in the hero).
keyCharacteristics: Industrial amber drench on the brand bar; void-black stage everywhere else
Hard edges (0 radius) on cards, bars, and chips; full borders, never soft chrome
Motorblock wordmark + Geist Pixel Square titles + Geist Mono body/labels
HUD density: compact orbit cards, edge-docked console, tight section rhythm
Flat / tonal elevation; amber glow only as operational feedback

rules.name: The Amber Signal Rule
rules.body: Industrial amber is identity on the brand bar and a sparse operational signal on the black stage. Never wash large dark regions in amber gradients or purple-indigo substitutes.

rules.name: The No Pure Black/White Rule
rules.body: Prefer void black and phosphor white over #000 / #fff for large surfaces and body text. Selection may use mixed amber; UI chrome may use white opacity for borders only.

rules.name: The Brand-First Rule
rules.body: On the first viewport, HARDWIRE must remain a hero-level signal. No headline may overpower the wordmark or amber bar.

rules.name: The Instrumentation Rule
rules.body: Prefer Pixel Square + Mono for UI chrome. Do not introduce Inter, Roboto, or editorial serifs.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces rest flat. If a shadow appears without an active or focus state, remove it.

dos: Do keep the first viewport as one composition: brand, tagline, amber drench, and the globe stage.
Do use industrial amber as the committed identity color on the brand bar and as sparse HUD signals above it.
Do use hard edges (0 radius) and full borders on cards and consoles.
Do set body/label type in Geist Mono and titles in Geist Pixel Square; reserve Motorblock for HARDWIRE / mission display.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion for globe spin, ripple, and radar choreography.
Do keep summary copy sparse, exact, and evidence-first (defense-industrial voice).
donts: Don't use soft SaaS marketing: purple gradients, rounded card grids, or friendly illustrations.
Don't drift into neon cyberpunk / Matrix cosplay.
Don't adopt editorial magazine / Klim-specimen landing aesthetics (display serif + hairline rules as the brand).
Don't ship "AI wrapper" consumer product page patterns.
Don't add playful startup whimsy: emojis, pill clusters, or stat strips in the hero.
Don't place detached badges, promo stickers, or callout chips on top of hero media.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left / border-right >1px) as a colored accent pattern; use full borders or leading numerals.
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism stacks, or multi-layer marketing shadows.
Don't shrink the mobile emblem below ~2rem height or inflate the mobile brand bar with unnecessary vh padding.
